We're just on the same.
Hi.
Hey.
Hey, good morning guys.

has, has there's been some sort of changes within the org that is kind of driven the, you know, the need to kind of reopen the door to the conversation.

Um what we had talked about.

I thought maybe you had spoken with her to schedule it.
So.

Does that sound like a good plan?
Yeah, that sounds great.
Um And we've got extra ears on the call too so that can help me relay some information.
Um If we feel like it's a good fit.
Yeah, perfect, perfect.
Let's start off learning a little bit more about your current market serving process.
What does that, what does that look like internally?
And typically each property does their own market survey for their competitors in their area?

Ok.
What is exactly what does that process look like?
I mean, is it sort of a manual process like going to websites and calling around or are you guys, you know, utilize any tools internally to enable that process for you guys?

Nice.

Yeah.

and the least percentage
too, the least percentage.

ok, no worries.

How, what's the process look like for you guys going about pricing those units?

It just kind of depends on everybody's occupancy.
So that's really changed recently.
I mean, it was, it was steadily like, $10 every other week for a while.
But I mean, again right now, um, I'm sure, you know, it's, it, it, we're in a little bit tougher market so we just base it off of everybody's occupancy and we look at it weekly.
It's a way to balance supply and demand, totally get it.

I'm not familiar with it at all, honestly.

Did you guys have access to sort of a trial account or when?

I, I don't know the date.
Exactly.

Ok.
Awesome.

This data is updated on a daily basis and it's a fully automated platform.
So no manual process whatsoever.

No, I don't think so.
Justin,
do you have any curiosities?

for the most part.
No.

if we do what, I'm sorry.

So this uh and I'll explain this just for transparency, but this again has to do with how we track every single unit every single night.
So if unit 123 is available on the property website today, it's gonna fall in this available units bucket.
If tomorrow unit 123 is gone from the property website, we're going to assume that somebody put an application on that unit.
So we're gonna move it from the available units to the applications bucket.
If after seven days, that unit does not reappear on the property website, meaning there wasn't a cancellation denial, we're gonna move it from applications over to leases.
So that's kind of the science behind um you know, reporting these performance metrics off of uh public data.
Gotcha.
